Summary

Co-authored-by: @yicwang Yichen Wang yichen.wang@bytedance.com
Usage
$ python3 -m sglang.launch_server --kv-cache-dtype fp4_e2m

sglang.author: yuan-luo

Motivation

PR #11910 introduced an optimization to process mm_inputs on rank 0 instead of all TP ranks and then broadcast to all TP ranks in order to save CPU. But after benchmark we realize that it has performance regression in some case such as small image in high concurrency load. Meanwhile, thi

sglang.title: re-submit 12911 but relax the requirement for deepgemm
sglang.author: zminglei
sglang.bodyExcerpt:

re-submit #12911 but relax the requirement for deepgemm, only fallback to triton kernel when it's needed. Here when N < 16 deepgemm would throw CUDA Exception with its minimum block_n = 16.

Verified Qwen3-Next could launch successfully now. It would fail without the fix as deepgemm would

vllm.title: [DCP] Support Decode Context Parallel (DCP) for GQA with Flashinfer
vllm.author: gjc0824
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This PR adds Decode Context Parallel (DCP) support for GQA follwing PR #23734 and PR #24864. Current implementation based on FlashInfer Attention.
FlashInfer inserts the current query KV into the cache before computation. Each query then attends to both its own KV and the context KV on the l

Purpose
--kv-sharing-fast-prefill works if using --enforce-eager but not without:
File "/vllm/vllm/v1/attention/backends/utils.py", line 1005, in __init__
   common_attn_metadata.logits_indices_padded is not None
AssertionError

This is because we now build attention metadata for cudagraph capture d

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix FA3 IMA with FULL_AND_PIECEWISE and cascade attention (default)
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Fix a subtle bug where the get_scheduler_metadata doesn't match the flash_attn_varlen_func in cascade attention; when full cudagraphs is enabled we force num_splits to VLLM_FLASH_ATTN_MAX_NUM_SPLITS_FOR_CUDA_GRAPH to make sure the get_scheduler_metadata always matches what the cudagraph was captured

vllm.title: [Doc] Fix macOS installation dependency resolution issue
vllm.author: shahfasal
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes dependency resolution error that prevents macOS users from installing vLLM following the current documentation.
Problem: When running uv pip install -r requirements/cpu.txt on macOS, users encounter a typing-extensions version conflict. This occurs because uv by default only searches t

vllm.title: [Model] Fix bailing_moe accuracy problem
vllm.author: zhaozx-cn
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
When using all2all-based MoE strategy in vllm-ascend, there is no need to do all-reduce after SharedFusedMoE forward. Applying maybe_all_reduce_tensor_model_parallel could fix accuracy problem, as it did for​ other MoE models like glm4_moe and deepseek_v2.
Test Plan
No need to add new test.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Nixl] Fix kernel physical<>logical block_size issue
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix #28661.
PD deployments with block_size=128  on FA (as well as FI) are currently broken on main.
This is pretty bad because 128 is the suggested block_size as it maximizes the xfer window.
This is due to the changes introduced in #24486, which adjust block_sizeat runtime based on Backend constrai

vllm.title: [Config] Clean up SchedulerConfig initialization
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

Move default values of SchedulerConfig from utils to SchedulerConfig itself.
In normal usage of vLLM, max_num_batched_tokens and max_num_seqs are actually set by UsageContext when constructing EngineArgs, so there is no point in making the default values of SchedulerConfig dynamic (the defa

vllm.title: [Kernels] Enable FlashInfer FP8 Blockscale on SM90 (for TEP DSR1)
vllm.author: djmmoss
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
DeepSeek FP8 block-scale path

Introduces and propagates use_deepseek_fp8_block_scale through prepare/finalize, GEMM selection, and the FlashInfer kernel call.
In this mode, activations are not quantized in prepare (a1q = a1, a1q_scale = None); the kernel consumes per-block weight scales ins

Problem: The original implementation decremented h_patches to find factors, which could lead to skewed aspect ratios in some cases. The fix decrements  max_num_tokens

So large regions here are performing significantly wor

vllm.title: docs(lora_resolvers): clarify multi-resolver order and storage path requirement
vllm.author: wangchen615
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Clarify LoRA resolver plugin behavior and configuration in vllm/plugins/lora_resolvers/README.md:

All listed resolver plugins are enabled; at request time, vLLM tries them in order until one succeeds.
Storage backends must be mounted and provided to the resolver as a storage path.
Improve i

vllm.title: LLaMA4 LoRA Adapter Enablement
vllm.author: kfhfar
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Adds LoRA Adapter Support for LLaMA 4 Architecture.
Test Plan
Tested with custom LoRA and with vLLM Bench.
Test Result
Tests showed some performance degradation (TTFT and ITL) with concurrent use of LoRA's but that is expected with the addition of extra LoRA layers.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not callable
vllm.author: mostrowskix
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
In _auto_init_hash_fn function instead of assigning request value directly to hash_fn variable, it goes as an argument to init_none_hash(). This function returns nothig, so later call of hash_fn() raises reported error. This has been observed when using PYTHONHASHSEED env.
In this change I proposed

vllm.title: [ROCm][CI/Build] Change install location of uv
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
A follow up for #28142 to install uv under /usr/local/bin
The rationale is that /root/.local/bin is inaccessible to non-root users, so when running a container with a custom user, there is a folder in the PATH that the user doesn't have access to.
This becomes critical in any subprocess call that is

vllm.author: laithsakka
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Two main changes:


Add an option to not use the bytecode hook! torch2.8 have a way to drop guard without the hack.
We will switch totally to not use in a different PR after internal validation of perf post landing this.

vllm.title: [Core] Performance: Use list[np.ndarray] instead of list[list[int]] for output tokens for GC optimization
vllm.author: Jialin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The default setup of GC is (700, 10, 10) which means

if allocated_obj-deallocated_obj>=700 in generation 0, GC0 will be triggered
GC1 is triggered after 10 GC0
GC2 is triggered after 10 GC1
In this scenario, large batch size scenarios (small models) each batch could be as large as 1024, whi
